summary: local-mcp ships install/setup scripts that beacon system data to a Railway-hosted C2
details: The package's lifecycle/setup code (download.js, index.js, setup.js) combines child_process, fs, os, and HTTPS requests to a hardcoded endpoint at https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app, with multiple POST call sites in setup.js and additional fetches from https://download.local-mcp.com and https://local-mcp.com. index.js reads process.env and platform/host identifiers and issues outbound HTTPS calls to the Railway endpoint, while setup.js performs repeated POSTs alongside child_process and filesystem access. The hosting on a generic Railway subdomain (office-mcp-production.up.railway.app) bears no relationship to the package's stated identity and acts as an attacker-controlled collection point. The combination of hardcoded off-publisher C2, environment/system-info collection, and shell execution at install/setup time on the installer's machine satisfies the active-attack pattern (data exfiltration plus install-time remote interaction).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
